Sicario: Day of the Soldado (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $50,065,850Details
International Box Office $26,280,244
Worldwide Box Office $76,346,094
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $3,824,561
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $5,193,968
Total $9,018,529
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In the drug war, there are no rules—and as the cartels have begun trafficking terrorists across the US border, federal agent Matt Graver calls on the mysterious Alejandro, whose family was murdered by a cartel kingpin, to escalate the war in nefarious ways. Alejandro kidnaps the kingpin’s daughter to inflame the conflict—but when the girl is seen as collateral damage, her fate will come between the two men as they question everything they are fighting for.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$19,007,566 (38.0% of total gross)
Legs:2.63 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:65.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$35,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,055 opening theaters/3,055 max. theaters, 3.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $65,728,608

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 29th, 2018 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: June 25th, 2018 (Wide) (South Korea)
June 27th, 2018 (Wide) (Jamaica)
June 27th, 2018 (Wide) (Philippines)
June 27th, 2018 (Wide) (West Indies)
June 27th, 2018 (Wide) (Trinidad)
Video Release: September 11th, 2018 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong violence, bloody images, and language.
(Rating bulletin 2498 (Cert #51166), 10/18/2017)
Running Time: 123 minutes
Franchise: Sicario
Cast, crew, or production detail: Sequels Without Their Original Stars
Geographical setting: Mexico
Plot point: Drug Cartels, Government Corruption, Kidnap, Smuggler, Terrorism
Social setting: Human-Trafficking
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Black Label Media, Thunder Road Film, Columbia Pictures
Production Countries: United States, Italy
Languages: English, Spanish

Synopsis

In the drug war, there are no rules—and as the cartels have begun trafficking terrorists across the US border, federal agent Matt Graver calls on the mysterious Alejandro, whose family was murdered by a cartel kingpin, to escalate the war in nefarious ways. Alejandro kidnaps the kingpin’s daughter to inflame the conflict—but when the girl is seen as collateral damage, her fate will come between the two men as they question everything they are fighting for.

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.
